title: Frontier Mathematics Corpus Atlas
purpose: Machine-readable source, licensing, coverage and ingestion controls for building broad mathematics training, retrieval and evaluation corpora without treating free access as permission.
guardrails: Do not copy source content into this repository; the registry stores metadata, decisions and generated summaries only.
Free access, open access or dataset-level licensing never substitutes for origin-level rights.
Approved and conditional decisions do not by themselves establish universal legality of model training or output licensing.
Every acquired artifact must be immutable, hash-addressed and tied to the exact license evidence reviewed for that snapshot.
Share-alike, documentation-copyleft, code-copyleft and per-item sources remain in isolated shards with separate manifests.
Canonical benchmarks and competition problems default to evaluation-only or blocked to prevent contamination.
OpenStax current textbooks are blocked because the current pages explicitly prohibit LLM training/ingestion without permission.
No personal profiles, private data, deleted posts or unnecessary user identifiers may enter a corpus.
Any license change, takedown or upstream deletion invalidates the source decision until re-reviewed.
This atlas measures source coverage and governance readiness, not whether a resulting model is mathematically frontier-level.
